title: Methods and Research to Translate Principles into Practices
narration: The challenge: bridging the gap between AI principles and real-world practices requires structured methods.
Value Alignment ensures AI systems reflect human values through value-based design and value-aware adaptive systems.
Social Impact Assessment evaluates potential societal effects through stakeholder engagement and impact modeling.
Human-Centered Design puts people first through co-design collaboration and human-centered evaluation criteria.
Empirical Research studies AI effects on society through experiments, surveys, and interviews to gather evidence-based insights.
All four methods converge to bridge the gap, supported by research in behavioral change, social justice, and ethics governance.
elements: Value Alignment: value-based design, value-aware systems
Social Impact Assessment: stakeholder engagement, impact modeling
Empirical Research: experimental research, surveys and interviews
Convergence: bridging the gap with research areas and outcomes
connections: Social Impact Assessment -> Bridge the Gap
Bridge the Gap -> Research Areas + Governance + Outcomes
